ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flag; Iroas is Wizards-internal IP (Theros pantheon), no external crossover risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Appears in Commander Legends (CMR), released November 20, 2020 — the first Magic set designed for booster draft in the Commander format. Players drafted three packs, picking two cards per pick, then built 60-card decks.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: Commander Legends is a Draftable Commander Set Coming in 2020 (2019-10), via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Iroas in the card name is a direct reference to the Theros pantheon deity of victory and honorable warfare — patron of the polis of Akros. Per MTG Wiki, the Armory of Iroas is Iroas's canonical trophy site within Akros, making this card a named real place in Theros lore.
sources: MTG Wiki: Iroas, via WebSearch snippet
Scryfall: Iroas God of Victory JOU-150

trivia_bullets.text: Originally printed in Journey into Nyx (JOU-158, 2014-05-02) by artist Yeong-Hao Han. Commander Legends is its only reprint; both copies share the same art by the same artist.

"Each weapon is as unique as the champion who carried it to battle." Describes the votive-armory tradition of the Iroas cult in Akros — weapons of honored champions dedicated to the god rather than reused.
EDHREC rank 7,750 — niche equipment with a counter-on-attack loop. Legal in Pauper Commander; cited in EDHREC's Pauper Commander Bruenor Battlehammer equipment primer as a budget option.
sources: EDHREC: Pauper Commander Bruenor Battlehammer article, via WebSearch snippet
